compiled_state.title: Add Phase 0 comfort assessment pre-flight section to onboarding SKILL.md
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
UPDATE

## Implementation
Modify plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md to insert a new Phase 0: Comfort Assessment section before the current Phase 1: Auto-Detection.

File to modify: plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md

Phase 0 must include, in order:

### Step 0.1: Comfort Level Question (first user interaction)
Display a single question before any detection output:
```
Before we begin, I'd like to calibrate how I explain things.
Are you more comfortable with technical engineering details, or would you prefer plain-language explanations?

1) Technical — I'm comfortable with engineering terms, CLI commands, and configuration details
2) Non-technical — Plain language please; skip the jargon where possible

(Enter 1 or 2)
```

Record the answer as COMFORT_LEVEL: "technical" or "non_technical". This is the FIRST user interaction — it runs before detect-stack.sh output is shown to the user.

### Step 0.2: Stack and Project Auto-Detection
Run detect-stack.sh and project-detect.sh (same commands as Phase 1 Step 1, but now they run before showing anything to the user):
```bash
REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
DETECT_OUT=$(bash "$REPO_ROOT/.claude/scripts/dso project-detect.sh" "$REPO_ROOT" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
STACK_OUT=$(bash "$REPO_ROOT/.claude/scripts/dso detect-stack.sh" "$REPO_ROOT" 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
```

### Step 0.3: Confidence Level Assignment
Assign confidence levels (high/medium/low) for all 7 understanding areas based on detection output:

Assignment rules:
- stack: high if STACK_OUT is a recognized framework (not "unknown"); low if "unknown"
- commands: high if detect-stack output includes test/build commands; medium if test dirs found; low otherwise
- architecture: medium if multiple source dirs detected; low if no structure detected
- infrastructure: low by default (cannot be auto-detected from filesystem alone; requires user input)
- ci: high if CI workflow files found in .github/workflows/; low if none found
- design: medium if package.json with UI framework found; low otherwise
- enforcement: high if .pre-commit-config.yaml or .husky/ found; low otherwise

### Step 0.4: Initialize Confidence Context Object in Scratchpad
Write CONFIDENCE_CONTEXT to scratchpad (append-only):
```bash
echo "" >> "$SCRATCHPAD"
echo "## CONFIDENCE_CONTEXT" >> "$SCRATCHPAD"
echo "comfort_level: $COMFORT_LEVEL" >> "$SCRATCHPAD"
echo "detected_stack: $STACK_OUT" >> "$SCRATCHPAD"
echo "stack: $STACK_CONFIDENCE" >> "$SCRATCHPAD"
echo "commands: $COMMANDS_CONFIDENCE" >> "$SCRATCHPAD"
echo "architecture: $ARCH_CONFIDENCE" >> "$SCRATCHPAD"
echo "infrastructure: $INFRA_CONFIDENCE" >> "$SCRATCHPAD"
echo "ci: $CI_CONFIDENCE" >> "$SCRATCHPAD"
echo "design: $DESIGN_CONFIDENCE" >> "$SCRATCHPAD"
echo "enforcement: $ENFORCEMENT_CONFIDENCE" >> "$SCRATCHPAD"
echo "generated_at: $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ 2>/dev/null || date -u)" >> "$SCRATCHPAD"
```

The scratchpad CONFIDENCE_CONTEXT section is the single source of truth for all downstream phases. Downstream stories S2 (doc-folder scan) and S3 (routing) read and may elevate (but never lower) confidence levels. Schema contract: plugins/dso/docs/contracts/confidence-context.md.

Also update Phase 1's scratchpad initialization in Step 2: skip re-running detect-stack.sh and project-detect.sh (they already ran in Phase 0 Step 0.2). Reference the existing $DETECT_OUT and $STACK_OUT variables.

Update PHASE_PLAN initialization to include Phase 0 at position N=1 and shift all other phases:
```bash
echo "Phase 0: Comfort Assessment" >> "$SCRATCHPAD"
echo "Phase 1: Auto-Detection" >> "$SCRATCHPAD"
... (existing phases)
```

Update phase counter display rule: Phase 0 is position N=1 of Y where Y now includes Phase 0.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] test-onboarding-skill.sh passes after changes (structural marker tests from Task 1 are GREEN)
  Verify: bash tests/skills/test-onboarding-skill.sh
- [ ] test-onboarding-confidence-assignment.sh passes after changes (GREEN)
  Verify: bash tests/skills/test-onboarding-confidence-assignment.sh
- [ ] SKILL.md contains Phase 0 section before Phase 1 section
  Verify: awk '/Phase 0/{p0=NR} /Phase 1/{p1=NR} END{exit (p0>0 && p0<p1) ? 0 : 1}' plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md
- [ ] SKILL.md references CONFIDENCE_CONTEXT scratchpad section
  Verify: grep -q 'CONFIDENCE_CONTEXT' plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md
- [ ] SKILL.md references confidence-context.md contract
  Verify: grep -q 'confidence-context' plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md
- [ ] SKILL.md references all 7 dimension names in confidence context block
  Verify: for dim in stack commands architecture infrastructure ci design enforcement; do grep -q "$dim" plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md || exit 1; done
- [ ] First user interaction is the comfort question (Phase 0 appears before Step 0 dep scan)
  Verify: awk '/Phase 0/{p0=NR} /Step 0: Dependency/{s0=NR} END{exit (p0>0 && p0<s0) ? 0 : 1}' plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md
